items.title:
I'm going to make some obvious points.

(1) Blowing up all the oil infrastructure in the Middle East is an insane idea, and may well result in a global economic crash and humanitarian crisis unrivaled in the lives of those now living. We're talking about the price of everything everywhere rising, from food to gas, at a moment when inflation was already high. All of that will be laid at the feet of the authors of this war.

(2) The antebellum status quo of Feb 27, 2026 was just not that bad, but we're unlikely to return to it. Expect indefinite, long-term, ongoing disruptions to everything out of the Middle East.

(3) Also assume tech financing crashes for the indefinite future. The genius plan to get the Gulf states caught in the crossfire has incinerated much of the funding for LPs, for datacenters, and for IPOs. Anyone in tech who supported this war may soon learn the meaning of "force majeure" as funding gets yanked.

(4) Many capital allocators will instead be allocating much further down Maslow's hierarchy of needs, towards useful basic things like food and energy.

(5) It's fortunate that all those progressives yelled about the "climate crisis." Yes, their reasoning about timelines was wrong, and much of the money was wasted in graft, but the result was right: we all need energy independence from the Middle East, pronto. It's also fortunate that Elon and China autistically took climate seriously. Now they're going to need to ship a billion solar panels, electric vehicles, batteries, nuclear power plants, and the like to get everyone off oil, immediately.

(6) It's not just an oil and gas problem, of course. It's also a fertilizer problem, and a chemical precursor problem. Maybe some new sources will come online at the new prices, but it takes time to dial stuff up, particularly at this scale, so shortages are almost a certainty.

That said, China has actually scaled up coal-to-chemicals[a,c] (C2C), and there's also something more sci-fi called Power-to-X[b] which turns arbitrary power + water + air into hydrocarbons. But all of that will need to get accelerated. I have a background in chemical engineering so may start funding things in this area.

(7) Ultimately, this war is going to result in tremendous blame for anyone associated with it. It's a no-win scenario to blow up this much infrastructure for so many people. Simply not worth it for whatever objective they thought they were going to attain. But unless you're actually in a position to stop the madness, the pragmatic thing to do is: scramble to mitigate the fallout to yourself, your business, and your people.

[a]: https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/chinas-first-coal-chemicals-project-with-green-hydrogen-starts-commercial-2025-11-20/
[b]: https://www.alfalaval.com/industries/energy-and-utilities/sustainablesolutions/sustainable-solutions/clean-energy/power-to-x/
[c]: https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/climate-energy/chinese-alchemy-cheap-fuel-powers-coal-to-gas-chemicals-boom-2025-09-04/

(5) It's fortunate that all those progressives yelled about the "climate crisis." Yes, their reasoning about timelines was wrong, and much of the money was wasted in graft, but the result was right: we all need energy independence from the Middle East, pronto. It's also fortunate that Elon and China autistically took climate seriously. Now they're going to need to ship a billion solar panels, electric vehicles, batteries, nuclear power plants, and the like to get everyone off oil, immediately.

(6) It's not just an oil and gas problem, of course. It's also a fertilizer problem, and a chemical precursor problem. Maybe some new sources will come online at the new prices, but it takes time to dial stuff up, particularly at this scale, so shortages are almost a certainty.

That said, China has actually scaled up coal-to-chemicals[a,c] (C2C), and there's also something more sci-fi called Power-to-X[b] which turns arbitrary power + water + air into hydrocarbons. But all of that will need to get accelerated. I have a background in chemical engineering so may start funding things in this area.

(7) Ultimately, this war is going to result in tremendous blame for anyone associated with it. It's a no-win scenario to blow up this much infrastructure for so many people. Simply not worth it for whatever objective they thought they were going to attain. But unless you're actually in a position to stop the madness, the pragmatic thing to do is: scramble to mitigate the fallout to yourself, your business, and your people.

[a]: https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/chinas-first-coal-chemicals-project-with-green-hydrogen-starts-commercial-2025-11-20/
[b]: https://www.alfalaval.com/industries/energy-and-utilities/sustainablesolutions/sustainable-solutions/clean-energy/power-to-x/
[c]: https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/climate-energy/chinese-alchemy-cheap-fuel-powers-coal-to-gas-chemicals-boom-2025-09-04/
items.title_zh:
我将提出一些明显的观点。

(1) 炸毁中东所有石油基础设施是一个疯狂的想法，很可能会导致全球经济崩溃和人道主义危机，给人们的生活带来前所未有的影响。我们谈论的是在通货膨胀已经很高的时候，从食品到天然气，所有东西的价格都在上涨。所有这一切都将归咎于这场战争的制造者。

(2) 2026 年 2 月 27 日的战前现状并没有那么糟糕，但我们不太可能回到那样的状态。预计中东以外的一切都会受到无限期的、长期的、持续的干扰。

(3) 还假设科技融资在不确定的未来崩溃。让海湾国家陷入交火的天才计划已经烧毁了有限合伙人、数据中心和首次公开募股的大部分资金。随着资金被撤走，任何支持这场战争的科技界人士可能很快就会明白“不可抗力”的含义。

(4) 相反，许多资本配置者将进一步配置马斯洛的需求层次，用于有用的基本物品，如食品和能源。

(5) 幸运的是，所有进步人士都在大喊“气候危机”。是的，他们对时间表的推理是错误的，大部分资金都浪费在贪污中，但结果是正确的：我们都需要立即从中东实现能源独立。幸运的是，埃隆和中国认真对待气候问题。现在他们需要运送十亿个太阳能电池板、电动汽车、电池、核电站等，才能立即让每个人摆脱石油。

(6) 当然，这不仅仅是石油和天然气问题。这也是肥料问题和化学前体问题。也许一些新的来源会以新的价格上线，但需要时间来调配东西，特别是在这种规模下，所以短缺几乎是肯定的。

也就是说，中国实际上已经扩大了煤化工[a,c]（C2C）的规模，还有一种更科幻的东西，叫做Power-to-X[b]，可以将任意电力+水+空气转化为碳氢化合物。但所有这一切都需要加速。我有化学工程背景，所以可能会开始资助这个领域的事情。

(7) 最终，这场战争将导致任何与之相关的人受到巨大的指责。为这么多人炸毁这么多基础设施是双赢的局面。无论他们认为要实现什么目标，这根本不值得。但除非你真的有能力阻止这种疯狂行为，否则务实的做法是：努力减轻对你自己、你的企业和你的员工的影响。

[一]：https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/chinas-first-coal-chemicals-project-with-green-Hydrogen-starts-commercial-2025-11-20/
[b]：https://www.alfalaval.com/industries/energy-and-utilities/sustainablesolutions/sustainable-solutions/clean-energy/power-to-x/
[c]：https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/climate-energy/chinese-alchemy-cheap-fuel-powers-coal-to-gas-chemicals-boom-2025-09-04/

[c]：https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/climate-energy/chinese-alchemy-cheap-fuel-powers-coal-to-gas-chemicals-boom-2025-09-04/ / I'm going to make some obvious points.

We're talking about the price of everything everywhere rising, from food to gas, at a moment when inflation was already high. All of that will be laid at the feet of the authors of this war.

(2) The antebellum status quo of Feb 27, 2026 was just not that bad, but we're unlikely to return to it. Expect indefinite, long-term, ongoing disruptions to everything out of the Middle East.

(3) Also assume tech financing crashes for the indefinite future. The genius plan to get the Gulf states caught in the crossfire has incinerated much of the funding for LPs, for datacenters, and for IPOs. Anyone in tech who supported this war may soon learn the meaning of "force majeure" as funding gets yanked.

(4) Many capital allocators will instead be allocating much further down Maslow's hierarchy of needs, towards useful basic things like food and energy.

(5) It's fortunate that all those progressives yelled about the "climate crisis." But all of that will need to get accelerated. I have a background in chemical engineering so may start funding things in this area.

(7) Ultimately, this war is going to result in tremendous blame for anyone associated with it. It's a no-win scenario to blow up this much infrastructure for so many people. Simply not worth it for whatever objective they thought they were going to attain. But unless you're actually in a position to stop the madness, the pragmatic thing to do is: scramble to mitigate the fallout to yourself, your business, and your people.

items.title:
R to @RamboVanHalen: You’re talking about the late Soviets. By the Brezhnev era, I agree, they weren’t as psycho.

But the early Soviets, namely Lenin and Trotsky and Stalin, did a whole lot of evil that could fill dozens of movies.

Lenin’s Hanging Order
White Sea Labor Canal
The Russian Civil War
The Red Terror
Katyn Forest
Berlin Airlift

Just off the top of my head. All very dramatic stories that could become films.

items.title_zh:
R 对@RamboVanHalen：你说的是已故的苏联人。我同意，到了勃列日涅夫时代，他们就不再那么神经质了。

但早期的苏联人，即列宁、托洛茨基和斯大林，做了很多邪恶的事情，可以写满几十部电影。

列宁的悬而未决的命令
白海劳工运河
俄罗斯内战
红色恐怖
卡廷森林
柏林空运

就在我的头顶上。所有非常戏剧性的故事都可以拍成电影。

items.title:
R to @RamboVanHalen: Yeah, but streaming is a transitional form. It will likely be replaced by AI-generated video over time.

Streaming already competes with everything else on the Internet. You don’t commit to a two hour stream. Users click away quickly.

Moreover, AI video will reduce the cost of hiring new actors (who are often hard to manage) and increase the lifespan of old ones indefinitely.

So you’ll see Stallone and Schwarzenegger, in their prime, forever. They’ll be like Mickey Mouse.

In a real sense, American culture is now a “finished product”, like French culture. It’s not like France is innovating on baguettes and the Eiffel Tower.

Similarly, thanks to AI, you will see endless remixes of the past glory days of America, especially the 1980s, but also earlier eras. You already see that now on X. All the romanticized past. 

That’s part of why Hollywood is getting deprecated. You can only tell the same stories, the same sequels, with the same actors, to the same audience, so many times.

Meanwhile, all cultural innovation has moved to the Internet.

items.title_zh:
R 对@RamboVanHalen：是的，但流媒体是一种过渡形式。随着时间的推移，它可能会被人工智能生成的视频所取代。

流媒体已经与互联网上的其他一切竞争。你不会承诺两个小时的直播。用户快速点击离开。

此外，人工智能视频将降低雇用新演员（通常难以管理）的成本，并无限期地延长老演员的寿命。

所以你会永远看到史泰龙和施瓦辛格的巅峰时期。他们会像米老鼠一样。

从真正意义上来说，美国文化现在已经是“成品”了，就像法国文化一样。法国并没有在法棍面包和埃菲尔铁塔上进行创新。

同样，借助人工智能，您将看到美国过去辉煌岁月的无尽混音，尤其是 20 世纪 80 年代，但也包括更早的时代。你现在已经在 X 上看到了这一点。所有浪漫化的过去。 

这就是好莱坞被弃用的部分原因。你只能多次向同样的观众讲述同样的故事、同样的续集、同样的演员、同样的观众。

与此同时，所有的文化创新都转移到了互联网上。

items.title:
R to @balajis: I am sympathetic to @RamboVanHalen and his critique of KPI-driven clickbait. Obviously, there's truth to it.

On the other hand, for generations, Hollywood's blind spots were obvious. Thanks to the decentralization of media, new stories will get told. Eventually, some will become art.

https://reason.com/2000/06/01/hollywoods-missing-movies/

另一方面，对于几代人来说，好莱坞的盲点是显而易见的。由于媒体的去中心化，新的故事将会被讲述。最终，有些将成为艺术。
